title: gumira cumingiana
intro: Gumira cumingiana, also known as the Cuming's gumira, is a plant species belonging to the family Rutaceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. Traditionally, the plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat various respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Gumira cumingiana include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate coughs, and provide relief from digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are often used to make teas, infusions, and tonics, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
